chunk_summary: This section focuses on counselling strategies to address feeding difficulties and faltering growth, emphasizing practical approaches to improve nutritional intake and support healthy development in children.
learning_objectives: Understand the common causes of feeding difficulties and faltering growth in children.
Learn effective counselling techniques to support caregivers in managing feeding challenges.
Identify evidence-based strategies to improve nutritional intake and growth outcomes.
Recognize when to refer children with severe feeding issues to specialists.
key_facts: Faltering growth is defined as a failure to achieve expected weight gain in children, often falling below the 2nd percentile on growth charts.
Feeding difficulties affect up to 25% of typically developing children and up to 80% of children with developmental disorders.
Counselling should focus on creating a positive feeding environment, including structured mealtimes and minimizing distractions.
Nutritional interventions may include high-calorie supplements or fortified foods to meet caloric needs.
Parental anxiety can exacerbate feeding difficulties; addressing caregiver concerns is a key component of counselling.
Referral to a multidisciplinary team is recommended for severe or persistent cases of faltering growth.
reference_quote: ‘Faltering growth is best managed through a combination of nutritional interventions and caregiver education to ensure long-term improvements in feeding behaviors and growth outcomes.’
chunk_heading: FEEDING DIFFICULTIES & FALTERING GROWTH;COUNSELLING STRATEGIES FOR
